title: Using co-interior angles
problem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle measures $74°$. Find the other co-interior angle.

steps.instruction: Identify the relationship. Co-interior angles lie between the parallel lines on the same side of the transversal.
steps.working: The two angles are co-interior (also called same-side interior or allied angles).
Co-interior angles are supplementary: they add to $180°$.

title: Two-step angle problem with parallel lines
problem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. At the upper intersection, angle $a°$ and $128°$ lie on a straight line. Find the alternate interior angle $b°$ at the lower intersection.

steps.instruction: Find angle $a$ using angles on a straight line.
Apply the alternate interior angles property.
steps.working: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel, so $b = a = 52°$.

title: Solving an algebraic equation using co-interior angles
problem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The co-interior angles are $(4x + 10)°$ and $(2x + 20)°$. Find $x$ and the size of each angle.

steps.instruction: Write an equation using the co-interior angle property.
Find the size of each angle.
Check: co-interior angles must sum to $180°$.
